description: Excel double-subtracts this meter: it appears as a subtracted −term on row 26 (B310 formula) AND on row 30 (B314 formula). Ontology enforces one-parent-per-meter and attached it to B314 (shorter-formula-wins). B310 therefore over-counts vs Excel by Δ(B313.KV1_VMM22_V) each month (+120.82 MWh Jan-2026, +26.73 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable.

Excel lists B315.KV1_VMM21_V as both a +term on row 30 (B314) AND the sole +term on row 31 (B315). Ontology attached the meter to B315 (prefix-match convention). Result: B311 over- counts by +23.72 Jan / +15.22 Feb (via the pool share), and B314 under-counts by the same magnitude. B315 itself matches Excel exactly. Excel is immutable.
Device swap 2025-01-09 (last value 5386.99). Split A|B via rolling_sum.

Winter offline + catch-up flush 2025-12-16 → 2026-02-03 (47 days flat at 799.85 m³, then ramp up to 831 m³ by 2026-02-20). NOT an actual register swap — counter is monotonic, no reset event; the original "swap 2025-10-17" labeling was inaccurate (counter had already gone flat earlier). Patched via 3-segment slice/interpolate/slice + rolling_sum (:d.A | :d.patch | :d.B). Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference window for facit comparison, but the patch spreads the post-Feb-3 catch-up backwards into Dec/Jan — see excel_comparison_annotations classification as meter_outage.
Counter went offline 2025-10-17 at value 236425.72. Leaf meter; no children for sum-patch. Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference.

Counter went offline 2026-01-14 at value 278.83. Inside the Jan 2026 reference period — affects Jan 14 → Feb 28. Ref carries valid_to so downstream gets zero delta post-outage (flat-carry equivalent). Excel total not materially impacted (B301 is a small consumer).
Counter went offline 2026-02-24 at value 79.15. Inside Feb 2026 — last 4 days of the month missing.
Counter went offline 2026-02-21 at value 35.62. Inside Feb 2026 reference period.
Counter went offline 2026-02-10 at near-zero value (8.74e-05). Inside Feb 2026 reference period.
Counter went offline 2026-01-09 at value 77.94. Inside Jan 2026 reference period.
B311.KV1_VMM26 is the main kallvatten distribution node with 13 children. Currently healthy; candidate for sum-children patch if it ever goes offline.

All SNV KALLVATTEN building formulas cross-checked verbatim against reference/reference_formula_doc/the_only_one_formula_doc.xlsx (KALLVATTEN sheet). 39/40 buildings match current meter_allocations including the big B311 15-term pool, the B310 8-term pool, the B202 multi-intake chain, and every cross-building subtraction (B307 − B342 − B344; B327 − B326; B334 − B330; B339 − B318; B314 with + B315 − B313 term; B318 − B353 − B319). B336 is the only miss (see ann-snv-kv-b336-demolished). B204/205 and B330/331 combined in PME as documented elsewhere.

Reference formula doc lists a B336 formula (B334.KV1_VM26_V − (B392.KV1_VM21 + B392.KV1_VM22)) but marks it "Utgår byggnads rivs" (building being demolished). Ontology has no B336 allocation. Any residual B336 flow previously tracked by this transit-loss formula is unaccounted post- demolition. Confirm with facilities whether B392.VM21/VM22 are still expected to pass through B336 physically.

B217 KV formula updated on 2026-03-17 per reference formula doc ("FORMEL UPPDATERAD 2026-03-17"). Current formula is +B217.KV1_VM24_V only (single meter). Jan/Feb 2026 reconciliation windows pre-date this change; post-March 2026 values should continue to match.

B314.KV1_VM22_V was installed on 2025-04-22 per reference formula doc ("OBS B314.KV1_VM22_V installerades 2025-04-22"). Pre-install readings will be absent/zero, so the B310 formula term (−B314.KV1_VM22_V) contributes zero to the Jan-Apr 2025 window — expected, not a data gap.

B204 accounting in PME covers both B204 and B205 as one combined unit per reference formula doc ("B204/205 är det som gäller i PME"). B205 has no standalone KALLVATTEN formula; its consumption is embedded in B204.KV1_VM22_V.

B330 KV accounting covers both B330 and B331 as one combined unit per reference formula doc ("B330/331 är det som gäller i PME"). B331 has no standalone KALLVATTEN formula; its consumption is embedded in B330.KV1_VM23_V.

Counter flat for 115 days (2025-09-21 → 2026-01-13) at 5987.662, then single catch-up flush on 2026-01-14 (+1962.80 m³, ~17.1 m³/day across the window — consistent with pre-flat rate 15.7/day and post-flush rate 19.1/day). BMS gateway outage. A raw daily-delta view would show a spike on 2026-01-14 at B310 (+1963) and a mirror dip at B311 (−1963) since B310.KV1_VM23_V is a −term in B311's 15-term pool. Patched via 3-segment rolling_sum (:d.A slice valid_to=2025-09-21 | :d.patch interpolate 2025-09-21→2026-01-14 | :d.B slice valid_from=2026-01-14), same pattern as ann-snv-anga-b317-vmm72-offline-winter for ÅNGA.

Counter flat for 115 days (2025-09-21 → 2026-01-13) at 110.82, flush 2026-01-14 (+8.59 m³). Low magnitude but same BMS-gateway event as B310.KV1_VMM23_V. Patched via 3-segment rolling_sum with interpolate across the flat window. Same mirror-dip effect on B311's pool (B310.KV1_VM20_V is −term in B311).

Counter flat for 115 days (2025-09-21 → 2026-01-13) at 630.81, flush 2026-01-14 (+104.51 m³). Same BMS-gateway outage as B310.KV1_VMM23_V. Patched via 3-segment rolling_sum with interpolate across the flat window. Mirror-dip on B310 since B313.KV1_VM22_V is a −term in the B310 formula; also double-subtracted in Excel row 30 (B314) — see ann-snv-kv-b313-double-subtract.

Counter flat for 115 days (2025-09-21 → 2026-01-13) at 737.98, flush 2026-01-14 (+97.41 m³). Same BMS-gateway outage as B310.KV1_VMM23_V. Patched via 3-segment rolling_sum with interpolate across the flat window. Mirror-dip on B311 since B317.KV1_VM21_V is a −term in the B311 pool.

Counter flat for 48 days (2025-12-16 → 2026-02-02) at 943.09, flush 2026-02-03 (+261.16 m³, ~5.4 m³/day across the window). Separate event from the Sep→Jan winter outage (B389 is on a different BMS branch — brandvatten / fire-water). Patched via 3-segment rolling_sum with interpolate across the flat window. B389 appears only as a standalone +term in its own building formula, so no mirror effect elsewhere.

B302.KV1_VMM23_V flat at zero for the whole reference period (2025-01 → 2026-02). Reference formula doc's B302 formula uses VMM21, VMM22, VMM23 — so VMM23 is part of the building formula but currently reads zero. Either the sub-loop is idle or the counter is dormant. Low magnitude; accepted.

B304.KV1_VMM23_V had a 33-day flat run in early summer 2025 with low-magnitude readings (median 0.17 m³/day, max 24 m³/day). Seasonal / intermittent use pattern typical of auxiliary cold- water loops.

B308.KV1_VMM23_V shows a 5-day burst (2025-03-10 → 14) of 200–366 m³/day, well above the 17 m³/day median. No ontology patch needed — monotonic counter, real consumption. Possibly a process test or seasonal filling; worth noting for trend baselines.
B318.KV1_VMM21_V shows a 2-day spike (160 + 116 m³) above its ~4 m³/day baseline. Monotonic counter, no patch needed. Likely a process event; flagged for context only.

TN-billed SNV KV intake = 23 310 / 19 893 m³ Jan/Feb 2026 across 6 AZ-side meters per Total SNV tab: B311.KV1_VMM26_V (TN 11065/73353516) and B334.KV1_VMM21 (TN 23772633) — already in ontology as building meters; B217.KV1_VM001 (TN 68844912 brunn 1), B217.KV1_VM002 (TN 68844913 brunn 2), B390.KV1_VM25_V (TN 53989390), B409.KV1_VM21 (TN 23772635, currently offline) — 4 wells/inlets NOT in the ontology because the existing 30 root building-inlet meters already sum to ~TN total within 3 % (22 673 m³ Jan / 18 951 m³ Feb). The wells appear to be implicit upstream of existing root meters (would double-count if added). The 6 TN meters are billing reconciliation, not topology — SNV KV is distributed wells, not a substation pattern.
